annual-health-camp-2025.fullDescription: The Annual Community Health Camp is one of Karamsthali NGO's flagship initiatives, held every year to address the critical healthcare gap faced by underserved rural populations. In 2025, we aim to reach over 500 individuals across multiple villages in Rajasthan. The camp provides free medical consultations, blood pressure and sugar screenings, eye check-ups, dental hygiene awareness, and immunisation drives for children. Our partnerships with local government hospitals bring qualified doctors and nursing staff on-site. No registration or payment is required — services are completely free and open to all community members.
highlights: Free general physician consultations
Blood pressure, diabetes & haemoglobin screening
Eye check-up with free spectacles for eligible patients
Child immunisation and nutrition counselling
Dental hygiene awareness and dental kits distribution
Mental health awareness sessions by trained counsellors

women-empowerment-workshop-2025.fullDescription: The Women Empowerment & Skill Workshop is a transformative one-day programme designed to equip rural and semi-urban women with practical, income-generating skills. Participants receive hands-on training in tailoring, block printing, and handicraft production, alongside sessions on financial literacy, savings groups, and how to access microfinance. Karamsthali NGO's experienced mentors guide each participant through a structured curriculum. Lunch, materials, and certificates of participation are provided at no cost. By the end of the day, every woman leaves with both a new skill and a roadmap to implement it.
highlights: Hands-on tailoring and block printing workshops
Financial literacy and banking basics
Introduction to SHGs (Self Help Groups)
Guidance on obtaining microfinance loans
Take-home starter craft kits
Networking with established women entrepreneurs

education-drive-rural-schools-2025.fullDescription: The Education Drive targets government primary schools across the Bharatpur district where resource shortages are most acute. Our team of volunteers distributes essential educational kits — including geometry boxes, notebooks, pencils, erasers, school bags, and uniforms — to 1,000 children. Beyond material distribution, volunteer teachers from our network conduct interactive learning activities, storytelling sessions, and career awareness talks. The drive also identifies children who have dropped out of school and connects them with government reinstalment programmes.
highlights: Distribution of school bags, notebooks, stationery, and uniforms
Interactive learning activities and science demonstrations
Storytelling and creative arts sessions
Career awareness talks for upper primary students
Drop-out re-enrolment guidance for families
School infrastructure quick-fix support

tree-plantation-drive-2025.fullDescription: Celebrated on World Environment Day, the Green Rajasthan Tree Plantation Drive is Karamsthali's commitment to reversing environmental degradation in the Aravalli hills. Working alongside forest department officials, local school students, and community volunteers, we plant 5,000 native saplings — including Neem, Peepal, and Khejri — across identified barren patches. Volunteers also learn about soil conservation, watershed management, and the ecological significance of the Aravalli range. Every sapling planted is GPS-tagged and monitored for a full year.
highlights: 5,000 native saplings planted across Aravalli hills
GPS-tagging and year-long monitoring of each sapling
Guided awareness walk about local ecosystem
Soil health and watershed management demonstration
Certificate of participation for all volunteers
Free saplings for home planting distributed to participants

child-rights-awareness-campaign-2025.fullDescription: Every child has the right to safety, education, love, and dignity. Yet, millions of children in rural India remain unaware of these rights, and so do the adults around them. The Child Rights Awareness Campaign brings together local families, teachers, elected representatives, and children themselves for an afternoon of awareness activities. Through street plays, interactive games, testimonials, and expert discussions, the campaign builds a protective community network around every child. It is also an open platform for children to voice their concerns and aspirations.
highlights: Street plays and puppet shows on child rights
Interactive quiz games for children
Expert panel discussion on POCSO Act & child protection
Testimonials from child rights advocates
Information stalls on reporting abuse and getting help
Art corner where children express through painting

agenda.activity: Expert Panel: POCSO Act & Child Protection

annual-fundraiser-gala-2025.fullDescription: The Annual Fundraiser Gala is Karamsthali NGO's most prestigious event of the year — an elegant evening that brings together philanthropists, corporate leaders, government dignitaries, and passionate changemakers under one roof. The night features impact storytelling with live testimonials, cultural performances celebrating Rajasthani heritage, a silent auction of curated artworks by local craftspersons, and recognition of outstanding donors and volunteers. All funds raised directly support our flagship programmes in healthcare, education, and women's empowerment for the upcoming year.
highlights: Impact storytelling with live beneficiary testimonials
Cultural performances: Rajasthani folk dance & music
Silent auction of curated local artworks
Donor and volunteer recognition awards
Networking dinner with changemakers
Exclusive NGO annual impact report launch

agenda.activity: Welcome Address & Annual Impact Report Launch

digital-literacy-camp-2025.fullDescription: Access to digital skills is no longer optional — it is the foundation of future employment and civic participation. The Digital Literacy Camp is a 5-day intensive programme for 200 rural youth between the ages of 14–22. Participants are taught basic computing, touch-typing, internet research, email communication, and introductory coding using Scratch and Python. Sessions also cover digital safety, understanding misinformation, and using digital tools for entrepreneurship. The camp is conducted at Karamsthali's fully equipped tech hub in Ajmer, with laptops and internet access provided free of charge.
highlights: Basic computing and touch-typing fundamentals
Internet research and safe browsing practices
Email communication and office productivity tools
Introduction to Scratch and Python programming
Digital safety and identifying misinformation
Using e-commerce and digital platforms for business

agenda.activity: Office Tools: Word, Excel, and Presentations
Introduction to Coding with Scratch & Python
